theory.canonical_statement: A resonance mapping equates an acoustic harmonic frequency with the frequency associated with a Kaluza--Klein mode energy.
theory.technical_interpretation: There exists a resonance mapping between acoustic harmonics and Kaluza--Klein modes defined by \[ \omega_n = \frac{E_m}{\hbar}, \qquad \omega_n = n f_{\mathrm{fundamental}}, \qquad E_m = \frac{m\hbar c}{R_{\mathrm{KK}}}. \] Resonant access occurs when the acoustic harmonic frequency matches the Kaluza--Klein excitation energy.

equation_progression.interpretive_role: The acoustic carrier frequency is indexed by its harmonic mode.
The Kaluza--Klein excitation energy is scaled by compactification radius and mode index.
Matching the two sectors produces the resonance-selection condition.

derivation_layer.source_latex:
% BEGIN TZP DERIVATION LAYER
\tzpsection{Derivation Layer}
\paragraph{Primary Equation.}
\[
\omega_n=\frac{E_m}{\hbar},\qquad \omega_n=n f_{\mathrm{fundamental}},\qquad E_m=\frac{m\hbar c}{R_{\mathrm{KK}}}.
\]
\paragraph{Resonance Derivation.}
Substitute the compact-mode energy into the frequency matching condition:
\[
\omega_n=\frac{1}{\hbar}\frac{m\hbar c}{R_{\mathrm{KK}}}=\frac{m c}{R_{\mathrm{KK}}}.
\]
Equating this with the acoustic harmonic gives
\[
n f_{\mathrm{fundamental}}=\frac{m c}{R_{\mathrm{KK}}},\qquad f_{\mathrm{fundamental}}=\frac{m c}{n R_{\mathrm{KK}}}.
\]
\paragraph{Interpretation.}
The entry supplies a mode-matching condition; transfer remains conditional on a physical coupling mechanism and is not asserted by the algebra alone.
% END TZP DERIVATION LAYER

review_source_enrichment.method: equation-aligned PDF evidence followed by manual review draft

symbol_definitions.\\omega_n: angular frequency of the acoustic harmonic mode
symbol_definitions.E_m: energy of the indexed Kaluza--Klein mode
symbol_definitions.\\hbar: reduced Planck constant converting energy to angular frequency
symbol_definitions.c: speed of light in the compact-mode energy relation
symbol_definitions.R_{\\mathrm{KK}}: compactification radius setting the mode-energy spacing

dictionary_sections.definition: The dimensional access ladder is a resonance-matching operator between an acoustic harmonic and a Kaluza--Klein excitation mode. The source relation equates acoustic angular frequency with mode energy measured in frequency units.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: An acoustic carrier is indexed by \(n\), while a compact-dimension excitation is indexed by \(m\). When their frequency and energy spacing agree, the source describes a resonant channel through which the modeled domains may exchange information or energy.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: Substitution yields \(n f_{\mathrm{fundamental}}=m c/R_{\mathrm{KK}}\). Thus the selected acoustic frequency is conditioned by both mode indices and the compactification radius; decreasing \(R_{\mathrm{KK}}\) raises the matching scale for fixed indices.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: The retrieved source explicitly gives all three displayed relations and describes the match as a resonance mapping between acoustic harmonics and Kaluza--Klein modes. It identifies frequency matching as the trigger for the proposed access channel.
dictionary_sections.technical_interpretation: The equation is a selection condition rather than proof of transfer efficiency. It defines which acoustic excitation is kinematically aligned with a compact-mode energy spacing before any coupling strength is evaluated.
dictionary_sections.equation_grounding: \(\omega_n\) is the angular frequency of the acoustic harmonic; \(E_m\) is the Kaluza--Klein mode energy; \(f_{\mathrm{fundamental}}\) is the fundamental acoustic frequency; \(R_{\mathrm{KK}}\) is compactification radius; and \(n,m\) identify the corresponding modes.
dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: This entry connects bounded acoustic harmonics to the compact-sector progression by providing a precise resonance condition rather than a general analogy.

tzp_thesaurus.meaning: A matching condition equating the frequencies associated with two modeled sectors.
An excitation indexed in the compact extra-dimensional sector.
The radius scale controlling the spacing of compact-sector mode energies.

review_publication_overrides.equation_support_summary: Combining the acoustic harmonic frequency with the Kaluza--Klein energy spacing gives the resonance selection rule \(n f_{\mathrm{fundamental}}=m c/R_{\mathrm{KK}}\).
review_publication_overrides.proof_latex:
\textbf{Statement:} The source supplies the resonance relations
\[
\omega_n=\frac{E_m}{\hbar},\qquad \omega_n=n f_{\mathrm{fundamental}},\qquad E_m=\frac{m\hbar c}{R_{\mathrm{KK}}}.
\]
\textbf{Logic Validation:}\\
\textbf{Proposition:} A mode pair is selected when its acoustic frequency equals its compact-sector frequency.\\
\textbf{Proof:} Substituting \(E_m=m\hbar c/R_{\mathrm{KK}}\) into \(\omega_n=E_m/\hbar\) gives \(\omega_n=m c/R_{\mathrm{KK}}\). Equating this with \(\omega_n=n f_{\mathrm{fundamental}}\) gives \(n f_{\mathrm{fundamental}}=m c/R_{\mathrm{KK}}\).\\
\textbf{VERDICT:} \ensuremath{\checkmark}\ \textbf{TRUE} --- algebraic resonance selection follows from the displayed relations\\
\textbf{Formal Status:} \ensuremath{\checkmark}\ THEORETICAL -- source-backed CHL obligation\\
\textbf{Physical Status:} THEORETICAL -- coupling efficiency remains to be tested
